2604.10529.title: AI Patents in the United States and China: Measurement, Organization, and Knowledge Flows
2604.10529.paper_summary: We develop a high-precision classifier to measure artificial intelligence (AI) patents by fine-tuning PatentSBERTa on manually labeled data from the USPTO's AI Patent Dataset. Our classifier substantially improves the existing USPTO approach, achieving 97.0% precision, 91.3% recall, and a 94.0% F1 score, and it generalizes well to Chinese patents based on citation and lexical validation. Applying it to granted U.S. patents (1976-2023) and Chinese patents (2010-2023), we document rapid growth in AI patenting in both countries and broad convergence in AI patenting intensity and subfield composition, even as China surpasses the United States in recent annual patent counts. The organization of AI innovation nevertheless differs sharply: U.S. AI patenting is concentrated among large private incumbents and established hubs, whereas Chinese AI patenting is more geographically diffuse and institutionally diverse, with larger roles for universities and state-owned enterprises. For listed firms, AI patents command a robust market-value premium in both countries. Cross-border citations show continued technological interdependence rather than decoupling, with Chinese AI inventors relying more heavily on U.S. frontier knowledge than vice versa.
2604.10529.paper_summary_zh: 我們開發了一個高精度的分類器，以通過對美國專利商標局（USPTO）的人工智慧專利數據集中的手動標記數據進行微調來測量人工智慧（AI）專利。我們的分類器顯著改善了現有的USPTO方法，達到了97.0%的精確度、91.3%的召回率和94.0%的F1分數，並且基於引用和詞彙驗證對中國專利具有良好的泛化能力。將其應用於授予的美國專利（1976-2023）和中國專利（2010-2023），我們記錄了兩國在AI專利方面的快速增長，以及AI專利強度和子領域組成的廣泛趨同，即使中國在最近的年度專利數量上超越了美國。然而，AI創新的組織方式卻有著明顯的差異：美國的AI專利主要集中在大型私人企業和既有中心，而中國的AI專利則在地理上更為分散，並在制度上更為多樣，對於大學和國有企業的角色更大。對於上市公司而言，AI專利在兩國都能帶來強勁的市場價值溢價。跨境引用顯示出持續的技術相互依賴，而非脫鉤，中國的AI發明者對美國前沿知識的依賴程度高於反之。
2604.10529.author: Hanming Fang et.al.
2604.10529.authors: Hanming Fang, Xian Gu, Hanyin Yan, Wu Zhu
